channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: NVIDIA Partners with 51WORLD on L4 Autonomous Driving Simulation
item.description: 51WORLD partners with NVIDIA to power next-generation L4 autonomous driving through simulation, reinforcing its leadership in the Physical AI infrastructure race.

item.title: Cloudflare Access experiencing delays with Access app creation and updates
item.description: Mar 18, 02:24 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 18, 02:19 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an issue causing configuration changes for creating, updating, and removing Access applications to be delayed. Existing Access applications are not...

item.title: Honda’s “Reverse Export” to Japan: A Struggling EV and China’s Changing Role
item.description: Honda is exporting its underperforming China-built EV back to Japan, highlighting both its struggles in China’s EV market and a broader strategic reset.

item.title: Nemotron 3 Nano 4B: A Compact Hybrid Model for Efficient Local AI
The Pentagon is planning for AI companies to train on classified data, defense official says
item.description: The Pentagon is discussing plans to set up secure environments for generative AI companies to train military-specific versions of their models on classified data, MIT Technology Review has learned.  AI models like Anthropic’s Claude are already used to answer questions in classified settings;...

item.title: Why Anthropic Thinks AI Should Have Its Own Computer — Felix Rieseberg of Claude Cowork & Claude Code Desktop
item.description: Claude Cowork came out of an accident.

item.title: Cloudflare Workers Issues
item.description: Mar 17, 21:11 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 16, 23:16 UTCMonitoring - We have identified and implemented a fix for this issue. We are currently monitoring the results.Mar 16, 21:41 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of errors for customers running...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 17, 20:02 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 17, 19:47 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: Excel Plugin is inaccessible for some users
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Connectors/Apps (Operational)

item.title: High Error rates with ChatGPT 5.4 Pro
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: AI Flaws in Amazon Bedrock, LangSmith, and SGLang Enable Data Exfiltration and RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new method for exfiltrating sensitive data from artificial intelligence (AI) code execution environments using domain name system (DNS) queries. In a report published Monday, BeyondTrust revealed that Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Code Interpreter's...

item.title: State of Open Source on Hugging Face: Spring 2026
Tech Giants Invest $12.5 Million in Open Source Security
item.description: Anthropic, AWS, Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI fund the Linux Foundation’s long-term security initiatives focused on open source software. The post Tech Giants Invest $12.5 Million in Open Source Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Mar 17, 15:45 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 17, 15:28 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 17, 15:21 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.Mar 17, 14:07 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is...

item.title: UK Companies House Exposed Details of Millions of Firms
item.description: The government agency confirmed the vulnerability could have been exploited to obtain company details and alter records. The post UK Companies House Exposed Details of Millions of Firms appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: LeakNet Ransomware Uses ClickFix via Hacked Sites, Deploys Deno In-Memory Loader
item.description: The ransomware operation known as LeakNet has adopted the ClickFix social engineering tactic delivered through compromised websites as an initial access method. The use of ClickFix, where users are tricked into manually running malicious commands to address non-existent errors, is a departure from...

item.title: Surf AI Raises $57 Million for Agentic Security Operations Platform
item.description: The company has announced its launch, backed by funding from Accel, Cyberstarts, and Boldstart Ventures. The post Surf AI Raises $57 Million for Agentic Security Operations Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Robotic Surgery Giant Intuitive Discloses Cyberattack
item.description: The company says some of its internal business applications were accessed after an employee fell victim to a phishing attack. The post Robotic Surgery Giant Intuitive Discloses Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: 174 Vulnerabilities Targeted by RondoDox Botnet
item.description: The botnet has increased its activity, peaking at 15,000 exploitation attempts per day, and taking a more targeted approach. The post 174 Vulnerabilities Targeted by RondoDox Botnet appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Holotron-12B - High Throughput Computer Use Agent
The Download: OpenAI’s US military deal, and Grok’s CSAM lawsuit
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Where OpenAI’s technology could show up in Iran  OpenAI has controversially agreed to give the Pentagon access to its AI. But where exactly...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 17, 11:45 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 17, 11:37 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 17, 10:02 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 17, 09:46 UTCInvestigating - We are currently...

item.title: AI is Everywhere, But CISOs are Still Securing It with Yesterday's Skills and Tools, Study Finds
item.description: A majority of security leaders are struggling to defend AI systems with tools and skills that are not fit for the challenge, according to the AI and Adversarial Testing Benchmark Report 2026 from Pentera. The report, based on a survey of 300 US CISOs and senior security leaders, examines how...

item.title: Konni Deploys EndRAT Through Phishing, Uses KakaoTalk to Propagate Malware
item.description: North Korean threat actors have been observed sending phishing to compromise targets and obtain access to a victim's KakaoTalk desktop application to distribute malicious payloads to certain contacts. The activity has been attributed by South Korean threat intelligence firm Genians to a hacking...

item.title: Xiaomi to Unveil Next-Gen SU7 With "Dragon Chassis" on March 19
item.description: Xiaomi founder Lei Jun has revealed the next-generation SU7 will feature the all-new "Xiaomi Dragon Chassis," a software and hardware-integrated intelligent chassis system designed to deliver "exceptional driving质感" when the vehicle launches March 19.

item.title: Logical Qubit Raises Tens of Millions of USD as Superconducting Quantum Computing Gains VC Attention
item.description: Zhejiang University-incubated superconducting quantum computing startup Logical Qubit has completed two funding rounds totaling hundreds of millions of RMB, signaling mainstream VC interest in quantum computing as the next frontier beyond AI.

item.title: AeroBand Raises Nearly $14M Series A to Expand Smart Instrument Lineup
item.description: Smart instrument brand AeroBand has raised nearly $14 million in Series A funding led by Matrix Partners China to accelerate global expansion and develop AI-powered digital instruments that lower the barrier to music creation.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Mar 17, 08:23 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. At this stage, only free Claude.ai users are being impacted.Mar 17, 08:04 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited Wing FTP Vulnerability Leaking Server Paths
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added a medium-severity security flaw impacting Wing FTP to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability, CVE-2025-47813 (CVSS score: 4.3), is an information...

item.title: Elevated errors for sign-in and account creation
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified that users are experiencing elevated errors for sign-ins and account creations. We are working on implementing a mitigation.Affected components Login (Operational)

item.title: Human Attribution of Causality to AI Across Agency, Misuse, and Misalignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.13236v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI-related incidents are becoming increasingly frequent and severe, ranging from safety failures to misuse by malicious actors. In such complex situations, identifying which elements caused an adverse outcome, the problem of cause selection, is a...

item.title: A Dual-Path Generative Framework for Zero-Day Fraud Detection in Banking Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.13237v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: High-frequency banking environments face a critical trade-off between low-latency fraud detection and the regulatory explainability demanded by GDPR. Traditional rule-based and discriminative models struggle with "zero-day" attacks due to extreme...

item.title: Benchmarking Zero-Shot Reasoning Approaches for Error Detection in Solidity Smart Contracts
item.description: arXiv:2603.13239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Smart contracts play a central role in blockchain systems by encoding financial and operational logic. Still, their susceptibility to subtle security flaws poses significant risks of financial loss and erosion of trust. LLMs create new opportunities...

item.title: Think First, Diffuse Fast: Improving Diffusion Language Model Reasoning via Autoregressive Plan Conditioning
item.description: arXiv:2603.13243v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) generate text via iterative denoising but consistently underperform on multi-step reasoning. We hypothesize this gap stems from a coordination problem: AR models build coherence token-by-token, while diffusion...

item.title: Automating Document Intelligence in Statutory City Planning
item.description: arXiv:2603.13245v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: UK planning authorities face a legislative conflict between the Planning Act, which mandates public access to application documents, and the Data Protection Act, which requires protection of personal information. This situation creates a manually...

item.title: Translational Gaps in Graph Transformers for Longitudinal EHR Prediction: A Critical Appraisal of GT-BEHRT
item.description: arXiv:2603.13231v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transformer-based models have improved predictive modeling on longitudinal electronic health records through large-scale self-supervised pretraining. However, most EHR transformer architectures treat each clinical encounter as an unordered collection...

item.title: RFX-Fuse: Breiman and Cutler's Unified ML Engine + Native Explainable Similarity
item.description: arXiv:2603.13234v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Breiman and Cutler's original Random Forest was designed as a unified ML engine -- not merely an ensemble predictor. Their implementation included classification, regression, unsupervised learning, proximity-based similarity, outlier detection,...

item.title: Continual Fine-Tuning with Provably Accurate and Parameter-Free Task Retrieval
item.description: arXiv:2603.13235v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Continual fine-tuning aims to adapt a pre-trained backbone to new tasks sequentially while preserving performance on earlier tasks whose data are no longer available. Existing approaches fall into two categories which include input- and...

item.title: Introducing Feature-Based Trajectory Clustering, a clustering algorithm for longitudinal data
item.description: arXiv:2603.13254v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a new algorithm for clustering longitudinal data. Data of this type can be conceptualized as consisting of individuals and, for each such individual, observations of a time-dependent variable made at various times. Generically, the specific...

item.title: Your Code Agent Can Grow Alongside You with Structured Memory
item.description: arXiv:2603.13258v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While "Intent-oriented programming" (or "Vibe Coding") redefines software engineering, existing code agents remain tethered to static code snapshots. Consequently, they struggle to model the critical information embedded in the temporal evolution of...

item.title: Slang Context-based Inference Enhancement via Greedy Search-Guided Chain-of-Thought Prompting
item.description: arXiv:2603.13230v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Slang interpretation has been a challenging downstream task for Large Language Models (LLMs) as the expressions are inherently embedded in contextual, cultural, and linguistic frameworks. In the absence of domain-specific training data, it is...

item.title: Steering at the Source: Style Modulation Heads for Robust Persona Control
item.description: arXiv:2603.13249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Activation steering offers a computationally efficient mechanism for controlling Large Language Models (LLMs) without fine-tuning. While effectively controlling target traits (e.g., persona), coherency degradation remains a major obstacle to safety...

item.title: Training-Free Agentic AI: Probabilistic Control and Coordination in Multi-Agent LLM Systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.13256v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent large language model (LLM) systems enable complex, long-horizon reasoning by composing specialized agents, but practical deployment remains hindered by inefficient routing, noisy feedback, and high interaction cost. We introduce REDEREF, a...

item.title: How Transformers Reject Wrong Answers: Rotational Dynamics of Factual Constraint Processing
item.description: arXiv:2603.13259v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When a language model is fed a wrong answer, what happens inside the network? Current understanding treats truthfulness as a static property of individual-layer representations-a direction to be probed, a feature to be extracted. Less is known about...

item.title: Explain in Your Own Words: Improving Reasoning via Token-Selective Dual Knowledge Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2603.13260v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knowledge Distillation (KD) can transfer the reasoning abilities of large models to smaller ones, which can reduce the costs to generate Chain-of-Thoughts for reasoning tasks. KD methods typically ask the student to mimic the teacher's distribution...

item.title: Elevated Errors Affecting ChatGPT Free and Guest Plan Users
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Conversations (Degraded performance)

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Amsterdam
item.description: Mar 17, 03:34 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 17, 03:26 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 17, 02:48 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Network PerformanceWe are working to analyze and mitigate this problem....

item.title: [AINews] NVIDIA GTC: Jensen goes hard on OpenClaw, Vera CPU, and announces $1T sales backlog in 2027
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on NVIDIA GTC 2026

item.title: INC0153706
item.description: Mar 16, 23:45 UTCIdentified - Current status: We've determined a recent improvement to Snowflake alerts creation and alteration contains a code issue, which is causing the impact. We've developed a fix and are rolling it out to restore service. We'll provide another update within 6 hours.Customer...

item.title: Elevated R2 Error Rate in India
item.description: Mar 16, 22:48 UTCResolved - We were able to identify and apply an effective fix for this issue. This incident is now resolved.Mar 16, 21:56 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating an issue where customers accessing R2 from India may see high error rates and increased latency for their R2...
